topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink swam on the paper, a mocking testament to my utter failure. A C- on the history essay. I slammed my textbook shut so hard the spine cracked. My hands were shaking, a frantic tremor that had nothing to do with the cold in the room. "This is ridiculous," I muttered, the words tasting like ash in my mouth. I paced the small confines of my dorm room, the cheap carpet muffling my angry footsteps. Every textbook, every note, felt like a deliberate betrayal. I glared at the ceiling, a tight knot tightening in my chest. I needed to rip the paper up, to destroy the evidence of this profound injustice.

[story 2]

I stared at the transcript, my breath catching in a sharp, shallow gasp. The number staring back at me was an insult. It was a significant drop from what I had painstakingly poured into that assignment. A slow burn started in my stomach, a heat that quickly escalated into a tight, buzzing energy. I picked up my pen, not to write, but just to grip it until my knuckles were white. I let out a sound, half-choked laugh, a harsh, ugly noise that bounced off the bare walls. "Unbelievable," I whispered, my voice dangerously low, vibrating with suppressed fury. I threw the pen down, the clatter echoing the sudden, violent rejection I felt toward the entire course.

[story 3]

My jaw clenched so hard I could feel the strain in my molars. The grade—a dismal D—was an absolute affront to my effort. I stood by the window, watching the indifferent traffic flow below, feeling a profound sense of injustice wash over me. My shoulders hunched, rigid and tense. I wanted to scream, to shout until my throat tore, but the sound caught somewhere behind my teeth. I turned abruptly, pacing back and forth across the worn floorboards. I kicked lightly at a stray sock, the small act of defiance fueling the churning sensation inside. "They don't understand," I hissed to the empty room, my voice tight with barely contained agitation.
